arts.ttl: Revealed: Manchester City's record without Rodri, as reports claim the superstar is set to be 'OUT for the rest of the season with a cruciate ligament rupture'
arts.sum: Manchester City could be without midfield lynchpin Rodri for the remainder of the season in a huge blow to their quest to win their fifth-straight Premier League title.

arts.ttl: What Leandro Trossard told Arsenal team-mates after sending off at Manchester City
arts.sum: Leandro Trossard became the second Arsenal star to be sent off for kicking the ball away over recent weeks and had some defiant words for his team-mates after his dismissal

arts.ttl: Arsenal fans argue reports of Rodri being out for the season are 'karma' after claiming the Man City midfielder 'tried to end Martin Odegaard's career' during international clash last year
arts.sum: Arsenal fans are showing little sympathy for Rodri following reports that he could be out of the rest of the season as they point to a challenge the City man made on Martin Odegaard last year.

arts.ttl: Former Man City star offers himself to Barcelona after Ter Stegen injury
arts.sum: Former Manchester City goalkeeper Claudio Bravo is willing to come out of retirement and play for Barcelona following Marc-Andre ter Stegen's cruciate ligament injury.

arts.ttl: 'Running the risk': Dermot Gallagher shares his verdict on Erling Haaland throwing the ball at Gabriel's head
Rodri could miss rest of season due to ACL injury suffered against Arsenal
arts.sum: City fear worst after midfielder went down in agony Absence would represent big blow to City’s title hopes Rodri suffered an anterior cruciate ligament injury in Manchester City’s 2-2 draw with Arsenal on Sunday and may miss the rest of the season as a result, handing the champions a serious blow in their bid to win a fifth successive Premier League crown. The 28-year-old was forced off after 21 minutes at the Etihad Stadium having gone down under minimal contact following a challenge by Thomas Partey inside the Arsenal area but in clear agony. City await further tests but fear the worst in regards to the availability of one of Pep Guardiola’s most important and influential players. As the manager said of the midfielder – who he rates as the best No 6 in the world – in March: “He is irreplaceable right now.” Continue reading...

arts.ttl: Arsenal's Gabriel breaks silence on Erling Haaland incident with Man City threat
arts.sum: Arsenal centre-back Gabriel Magalhaes warns Manchester City that the Gunners will be "waiting" for them at the Emirates following their fiery 2-2 draw at the Etihad.

arts.ttl: Gabriel response to Erling Haaland incident ridicules Arsenal reaction to Man City celebrations
arts.sum: Gabriel Magalhaes has broken his silence on the incident which saw Erling Haaland throw a ball at his head in the aftermath of Manchester City's late equaliser vs Arsenal.

arts.ttl: Ian Wright brands Erling Haaland a COWARD for Man City vs Arsenal antics
arts.sum: Ian Wright thought Erling Haaland "was better than that" after throwing the ball at the back of Gabriel's head after Manchester City's 98th-minute equaliser against Arsenal

arts.ttl: WATCH: Erling Haaland engages in war of words with Mikel Arteta and Gabriel Jesus
arts.sum: "What what what what?" Haaland responds, to which Jesus answers: "Why you saying, you talking?" "Come," Jesus says as he turns Haaland away from the Arsenal boss.
